business.headline: Urban Company share price jumps 15%. Why is it rising today?At around 10:06 am, the Urban Company was trading 13.25% higher at Rs 124.70. Earlier in the trading session, the stock was up around 15%.
Infosys, TCS, HCLTech: Why are IT stocks rising today?As of 9:49 am, major IT stocks such as Infosys, TCS and HCLTech were up around 1–3%, emerging among the key contributors to the Nifty's gains.

IDBI Bank share price jumps 4% after steep fall. What's driving the rebound?The stock had earlier fallen sharply after reports suggested that the government may revisit or restart the stake sale process due to lower-than-expected bids, raising concerns about delays in divestment.
Sensex opens 300 points higher, Nifty above 23,600; TCS up 2%The S&P BSE Sensex gained 456.82 points to 76,527.66, while the NSE Nifty50 added 144.25 points to 23,725.40 as of 9:30 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Stock markets to fall or rally today?Early indicators show a steady opening. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,655.5 at around 08:25 am, suggesting that the Nifty 50 may open slightly above Tuesday's close of 23,581.15.

Live: 7 FIRs in Faridabad over LPG theft and illegal gas refillingLPG crisis live updates: The government has said the LPG supply situation remains a concern and has urged consumers to use cooking gas judiciously, even as steps are being taken to expand piped natural gas infrastructure to reduce dependence on LPG. In a nationwide crackdown, officials said over 12,000 raids have been carried out and more than 15,000 LPG cylinders seized to curb hoarding and black marketing under the Essential Commodities Act. Authorities are also pushing commercial users in urban areas to shift to PNG, with easier connection processes and incentives being offered. Online LPG bookings have risen to around 94%, while commercial supplies have started resuming in several states with government support. Even as fresh shipments like Shivalik and Nanda Devi have reached India, the impact of earlier disruptions continues to be felt across sectors. Follow this live blog for the latest updates on LPG supply, government measures and the situation across cities and industries in India.

Bank penalties on low-balance accounts touch Rs 19,000 crore in 3 yearsBanks have collected around Rs 19,083 crore as penalties over the past three years. Out of this, public sector banks accounted for about Rs 8,092.83 crore, while the remaining amount was collected by private sector banks.

IRDAI revamps key advisory committee, brings in industry and policy expertsThe reconstituted panel includes several key institutional representatives. These include members from the Life Insurance Council and the General Insurance Council, along with heads of important professional bodies.
Sensex ends 568 points higher, Nifty above 23,500; Eternal up 6%The S&P BSE Sensex jumped 567.99 points to close at 76,070.84, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 172.35 points to end at 23,581.15.

LPG situation concerning: Government calls for judicious use amid shortage fearsThe Centre has urged judicious LPG use, pushed PNG adoption for commercial users, and asked states to fast-track approvals for city gas pipelines to ensure stable supply and avoid disruptions.

Your health insurance premium increased sharply? What's really behind itMany people are seeing sudden jumps in their health insurance premiums at renewal. The reasons aren't always clear, and insurers don't always explain them. Here's a closer look at what's behind it.

From ITR revision to office commute relief: 5 big tax changes from April 1Starting April 1, several tax rules are set to change in ways that could directly impact your filing and savings. From ITR revisions to commute benefits, here's a quick look at what's new.

GSP Crop Science IPO: should you subscribe to the public listing? Check GMPGSP Crop Science IPO is a book-built issue of Rs 400.00 crore. It includes a fresh issue of 75 lakh shares worth Rs 240.00 crore and an offer for sale of 50 lakh shares worth Rs 160.00 crore.

Should married couples be allowed to file taxes together in India?In most homes, finances are shared. But when it comes to taxes, couples are treated as individuals. Should that change? A new idea is now putting this question in focus.

How goal-based investing can transform savers into long-term wealth-builders?Goal-based investing reframes personal finance from a pursuit of returns into a pursuit of readiness. The most meaningful financial success is not the highest number on a statement, but the confidence that money will be available when life demands it.

Power stocks rise today; Adani Power gains 2% after 1,600 MW deal winThe buying interest was visible across the sector, led by stocks such as Tata Power, NLC India, Torrent Power and Adani Power. Among the top movers, Adani Power remained in focus after a fresh business development.

West Asia conflict triggers global helium shortage. Why it's no laughing matterCrude oil and LNG have been in the limelight amid the ongoing West Asia war. But another critical gas, helium, is now facing a global supply shock and here's why it's bad news.
Sensex, Nifty open flat as crude oil prices weigh in on investor sentimentThe S&P BSE Sensex gained 63.36 points to 75,566.21, while the NSE Nifty50 added 21.90 points to 23,430.70 as of 9:42 am.

Explained: Why gold prices are not rising despite ongoing Iran warIt is unusual for gold to fall during volatile situations like war. But that is exactly what is happening now and it raises a key question about what is driving prices today

Tenant vs landlord: How India's new rental rules change the balanceRenting a home in India has long been a game of negotiation, where rules were often unclear and power rarely equal between tenants and landlords. A new rental framework based on the Model Tenancy Act, 2021 is trying to formalise how homes are rented in India.

entertainment.headline: Karnataka women's panel flags Nora Fatehi song, seeks action over obscene lyrics The Karnataka State Commission for Women has raised serious concerns over the obscene lyrics and derogatory portrayal of women in Nora Fatehi's song from the film KD: The Devil, urging the CBFC to take strict action before certification.

Dhurandhar 2 is birth of new cinematic order; Spielberg should catch 1st show: RGV Ram Gopal Varma has doubled down on his praise for Dhurandhar 2 after watching the film, calling it the birth of a "new cinematic order." He even urged filmmakers like Steven Spielberg and Christopher Nolan to catch it first day, first show.

I am an orphan today: YouTuber Anurag Dobhal shares first post after car crash YouTuber Anurag Dobhal shared his first post after his car crash during an Instagram Live session. In an emotional note, Dobhal described it as the beginning of his new life.

Singer Maan Panu on quitting college, iPopstar journey and viral hit I'm Done Singer Maan Panu, who rose to fame with iPopstar, spoke about his rise to fame in an exclusive interview. He discussed his early challenges, experience on the singing reality show, his viral hit I'm Done and new track The Last Letter.

The White Lotus 4 expands cast with Kumail Nanjiani, Chloe Bennet, Jarrad Paul The makers of The White Lotus 4 welcomed new entrants to the series. Max Greenfield, Kumail Nanjiani, Chloe Bennet, Charlie Hall, and others have joined the cast.

Dhurandhar 2 cuts: Violent visuals deleted, demonetisation dates modified Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge has been certified A by the Central Board of Film Certification after edits. The film is set to premiere on March 18 and will have a wider release on March 19 during the Eid weekend.

Ustaad Bhagat Singh: Pawan Kalyan, Ugadi and the making of blockbuster magic Pawan Kalyan makes a much-anticipated return to Telugu cinema, with Ustaad Bhagat Singh releasing on Ugadi.  The film faces a direct box office clash with Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge, setting the stage for a major cinematic showdown.

Declare Sanatan faith in affidavit: Temple chief on Sara Ali Khan's Badri darshan The Badri-Kedar Temple Committee has introduced a new rule requiring non-Hindu visitors to submit an affidavit affirming belief in Hinduism to offer prayers at Badrinath and Kedarnath. The directive applies to all non-Hindu devotees, including actor Sara Ali Khan, a frequent visitor to Kedarnath.

Academy 'extremely upset' after Teyana Taylor calls out security at Oscars 2026 The Academy issued a statement after Teyana Taylor reported being manhandled by a security guard at the Oscars. Both the Academy and the security firm addressed the incident and promised to improve guest experience.

Dune: Part Three teaser trailer out, shows a darker and intense story The teaser trailer for Dune: Part Three was unveiled in Los Angeles, offering a first look at the film's key characters and its high-stakes narrative. Directed by Denis Villeneuve, the film follows Paul Atreides as he grapples with the consequences of power.

Inside Alia Bhatt's fun-filled 33rd birthday at Disneyland, actor shares dreamy pics Actor Alia Bhatt marked her 33rd birthday with a social media post, giving fans a look at private moments from her celebrations in Hong Kong with husband Ranbir Kapoor and their daughter Raha.

Ghost War trailer unveils new mission for John Krasinski Jack Ryan Jack Ryan: Ghost War brings back John Krasinski and main cast members from the Prime Video series for a new film installment, set to premiere on 20 May. Directed by Andrew Bernstein, the film continues the story of Tom Clancy's iconic CIA analyst.

Jane Fonda questions Barbra Streisand's Robert Redford Oscar tribute Jane Fonda questioned Barbra Streisand's Oscar tribute to late actor Robert Redford. Fonda said that she worked with Redford in four films, while Streisand only made one.

Radhikka Madan on Irrfan: He empowered me as an artist and woman Radhikka Madan recalled working with Irrfan in Angrezi Medium. She said he helped her feel confident and treated her as an equal on set.

Spider-Man Brand New Day: First look out; check out trailer release date The first footage of Tom Holland in Spider-Man: Brand New Day has been released. Directed by Destin Daniel Cretton, the film will hit theatres on July 31.

Oscar winner Octavia Spencer on crime against women, Lost Women of Alaska series Octavia Spencer discusses reframing true crime in Lost Women of Alaska, shifting the focus from perpetrators to the resilience of indigenous survivors while interrogating systemic neglect and racism.

Dhurandhar 2 gets A certificate, run time revealed Just two days ahead of its highly anticipated release, Dhurandhar: The Revenge has been granted an A certificate. The makers have also announced the film's final runtime.

Hansika Motwani visits Golden Temple after divorce, shares hopeful note Hansika Motwani visited the Golden Temple in Amritsar, sharing photos and a cryptic message reflecting optimism and resilience. She recently got divorced from businessman Sohael Khaturiya.

Rashmika Mandanna resumes Mysaa shoot after wedding; shares on-set glimpse Rashmika Mandanna resumed the shooting of Mysaa, following her wedding celebrations. She shared an on-set glimpse from the upcoming action-thriller.

general news.headline: Relief for flyers as government mandates 60% seats on flights be free of chargeAir travel rules are set for a shake-up as the government cracks down on hidden charges and pushes airlines to prioritise passengers. What changes for your next flight and what airlines must now do.

Ali Larijani killed along with son while at his daughter's home, confirms IranAli Larijani, a key architect of Iran's security and nuclear policy, was killed with his son in a strike, marking a major blow to the country's political leadership.

Who's winning the US-Israel-Iran war and how does it end?With the Israel-US-Iran war in its 18th day, the natural question is who's winning it. It is a difficult question to answer in an asymmetric war. However, we look at the targets of both sides and try to give a perspective of who is ahead in this war.
Ground Report: 22-storey building in Beirut flattened in massive Israeli strikeThe strike hit the central part of Beirut, which is home to the headquarters of several Lebanese ministries.
How Iran is puncturing Trump's bluster balloonThree weeks into Operation Epic Fury, the gap between Donald Trump's promises and Iran's reality is becoming impossible to ignore.

Iran regime not cracking, protesters will be slaughtered, Israel tells US: ReportIsrael urges Iranians to rise even as it privately fears a bloodbath. A leaked US cable reveals stark warnings, shifting US stance, and the risks facing protesters as the war enters a dangerous new phase.

US drops 2,200-kg bunker buster bombs on Iran missile sites near HormuzThe US military claimed that the strikes targeted 'Iranian anti-ship cruise missiles' that posed a direct risk to vessels moving through the Strait of Hormuz. The Central Command announced the bombings hours after Trump said most US allies had rejected his appeal for naval ships to escort merchant vessels through the strait.
A tale of two meetings: Karnataka Congress MLAs seek plum posts amid power tussleFollowing their dinner meeting, the MLAs decided to meet the Congress high command on March 26 in New Delhi.

The directive applies to all non-Hindu devotees, including actor Sara Ali Khan, a frequent visitor to Kedarnath.

Live: UAE scrambles fighter jets to intercept Iranian missiles, dronesUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East witnessed a sharp escalation on Tuesday, March 17, as Israel claimed it had successfully eliminated top Iranian security official Ali Larijani, who was widely believed to be running Iran since the killing of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Israel also said General Gholamreza Soleimani, the commander of Iran's Basij voluntary militia force, was among the other high-ranking officials killed in the fresh Israeli strikes. The deaths of both the officials were eventually confirmed by the Iranian state media by early Wednesday (IST). Meanwhile, an adviser to the UAE's president hinted that his country might join a possible US-led coalition to ensure safe passage of cargo ships through the Strait of Hormuz. Follow IndiaToday.in for live updates.

I'm alive: Netanyahu posts video with US envoy amid viral death rumoursThe latest video shows Netanyahu walking and chatting with US's Ambassador to Israel, Mike Huckabee, who joked that Trump had sent him to check on the Israeli leader.

Amid row, Nora Fatehi's KD song lyricist says he warned makers of censorship riskLyricist Raqueeb Alam has clarified that he did not write the controversial song Sarke Chunar featuring Nora Fatehi. Alam said he had only translated the Kannada version of the track, even though he had raised objections to it.

Four decades, same crisis: 40% of India's graduates still can't find jobsNearly four in ten young graduates in India are struggling to find jobs despite rising access to higher education, highlighting a deep mismatch between degrees and employability. A new report by Azim Premji University reveals how this persistent trend is raising concerns about the country's ability to fully benefit from its demographic advantage.

Little girl's 'Virat Kohli waala' jersey demand melts hearts online. Puma reactsA viral video of a little girl insisting on getting a "Virat Kohli waala" jersey has caught Puma's attention, with the brand asking the internet to help find her and gift an official jersey.

I am an orphan today: YouTuber Anurag Dobhal shares first post after car crashYouTuber Anurag Dobhal shared his first post after his car crash during an Instagram Live session. In an emotional note, Dobhal described it as the beginning of his new life.

American mercenary Matthew VanDyke, 6 Ukrainians arrested in India by NIASix Ukrainians and a US citizen have been arrested by the NIA for entering restricted areas in Mizoram, crossing into Myanmar, and engaging with insurgent-linked groups. The anti-terror agency has alleged that the group had plans to train militants and supply weapons, including drones, in a suspected cross-border terror network.

Space rock explodes with the force of 250 tons of TNT over OhioThe dramatic event startled residents across several states in the US, many of whom reported hearing a loud boom and feeling ground tremors.

Pak sleepwalked into Middle East maze, and 400 Afghans just paid for itAfter Pakistan signed the Strategic Mutual Defence Agreement with Saudi Arabia, it was obligated to help Riyadh amid Iranian drone and missile strikes in the Middle East. When Saudi Arabia needed military help and assurances, Pakistan carried out intensive bombing in Afghanistan to signal that it was already caught up in a war.

OpenAI says unlimited AI at current prices just doesn't make senseOpenAI's head of ChatGPT, Nick Turley, has claimed that the AI startup may rethink its unlimited subscription plan. Turley believes that having an unlimited AI plan would be similar to having unlimited electricity, something that may not make sense. His comments come after OpenAI CEO Sam Altman hinted at selling intelligence as a utility in the future.

sports.headline: King returns to protect the crown, as Kohli touches down in Bengaluru for IPL 2026 IPL 2026: The king is back. Virat Kohli has touched down in Bengaluru ahead of IPL 2026, ready to lead RCB's title defence.

India vs Pakistan set for Men's Hockey World Cup 2026, arch-rivals grouped together India are all set to face off against Pakistan as the arch-rivals have been grouped together for the upcoming Men's Hockey World Cup 2026. The Indian women's team will face China, England and South Africa in their group for the Women's Hockey World Cup.

Vinicius Jr hits brace as Real Madrid dump 10-man City out of Champions League A brace from Vinicius Jr. was enough for Real Madrid to dump 10-man Manchester City out of the Champions League and book their place in the quarterfinals. Arsenal and PSG progressed while Sporting dispatched Bodo/Glimt and completed a sensational comeback in the tie.

Senegal lose AFCON title in bizarre decision overturn, Morocco crowned winners Morocco has been awarded the 2026 African Cup of Nations title after the Confederation of ‌African Football (CAF) overturned the final win of Senegal. The CAF ruled that Senegal had forfeited their game after they left the field during the final following a controversial penalty being awarded to Morocco.

Riyan Parag Interview: Rajasthan captain looks to Premier League for inspiration Riyan Parag looks beyond cricket for leadership cues, drawing inspiration from West Ham's fight and Premier League resilience in the relegation battle as he prepares to lead Rajasthan Royals into a new era.

Emma Raducanu pulls out of Miami Open, continues struggling in challenging season British tennis star and 2021 US Open champion Emma Raducanu has withdrawn from the 2026 Miami Open due to illness and lingering post-viral symptoms, missing a chance to defend her 215 ranking points.

Did Kamran Akmal call Mohsin Naqvi a trophy chor after Bangladesh series loss? Former Pakistan wicketkeeper Kamran Akmal slammed the team's poor performance in the series against Bangladesh, calling Pakistan cricket a comedy series. His comments also indirectly referenced PCB chairman Mohsin Naqvi, linked to the Asia Cup controversy.

Pratika Rawal recalls emotional ride in Women's World Cup final: Didn't see last ball Pratika Rawal described the Women's World Cup final against South Africa as an emotional whirlwind, saying she didn't even watch the last ball. The right-handed batter missed the final due to an ankle and knee injury.

Mexico willing to host Iran's matches, after team refuses to play in USA Mexico is willing to host Iran's FIFA World Cup 2026 matches after the West Asian nation expressed reluctance to play in the United States amid its ongoing conflict involving Israel. The Mexican President said the country would have no problem hosting the games.

No more impact sub, but Rohit might spend time on the bench, hints MI coach IPL: Senior Mumbai Indians batter Rohit Sharma will not be used as an impact substitute this season, head coach Mahela Jayawardene said during a media interaction on March 17.

Australia cricketers in doubt for PSL 2026? PCB opens up on security concerns report The PCB has dismissed concerns over overseas players' travel for Pakistan Super League 2026, insisting all arrangements are in place and Australian players who have signed up will begin arriving next week.

Pak-origin cricketer hits back at Gavaskar after Abrar Ahmed's signing in The HundredAzeem Rafiq hits out at Sunil Gavaskar over his remarks on Abrar Ahmed's Hundred signing, calling them "ridiculous" as the controversy around IPL-linked ownership and Pakistan players grows.

MCC criticises Salman Agha for bizarre run out against Bangladesh, backs Mehidy HasanMCC clarified that Salman Agha's run-out in the second ODI against Bangladesh in Mirpur was fully valid under the Laws, stating he was clearly out of his crease while the ball remained in play.

R Ashwin questions fan army agendas, slams superhero culture in Indian cricketR Ashwin slammed the growing influence of fan armies and superhero culture in Indian cricket, saying the game is increasingly being overshadowed by PR-driven narratives, brand-building, and personality-focused debates.

Conway sparks carnage as New Zealand crush South Africa to win 2nd T20I by 68 runsDevon Conway's 60 and a fiery pace display from Ben Sears and Lockie Ferguson helped New Zealand crush South Africa by 68 runs, levelling the T20I series after a dramatic collapse.

Sensational Amelia Kerr scripts history with 100 Women's T20I wicketsNZW vs SAW: New Zealand skipper Amelia Kerr became the fastest cricketer from a full-member nation to complete 1,000 runs and 100 wickets in Women's T20Is, reaching the milestone in her 94th match against South Africa.

Experience then, fiery passion now: Suryakumar Yadav on India's evolution in T20IsT20 World Cup: Suryakumar Yadav compares India's 2024 and 2026 T20 World Cup wins, highlighting the shift from experienced composure to youthful intensity and how home pressure shaped the title defence.

KKR handed major scare as Harshit Rana set to miss most of IPL 2026IPL 2026: Kolkata Knight Riders have been handed a big injury scare. Fast bowler Harshit Rana, who was set to lead the team's attack, is most likely to miss most of the tournament. According to reports, Rana is not going to recover in time from his knee injury.

IPL 2026 injury worries grow: Cummins, Hazlewood doubtful for opening gamesSunrisers Hyderabad captain Pat Cummins and RCB pacer Josh Hazlewood are doubtful for the opening games of IPL 2026. Both Australian quicks are still awaiting medical clearance.

FIFA World Cup to suffer same fate as cricket? Iran urges games to be moved out of USAIran's football federation is pushing to move its 2026 World Cup matches from the US to Mexico over safety fears, in a controversy drawing comparisons to Bangladesh's T20 World Cup crisis.

Tilak Varma felicitated by Telangana CM Revanth Reddy after T20 World Cup triumphIndia T20 World Cup winner Tilak Varma received a warm welcome in his home state after the tournament triumph. Telangana Chief Minister A Revanth Reddy felicitated the young batter for his role in India's historic title defence.

Is CSK forcing a spot for MS Dhoni? De Villiers puzzled by veteran's role in IPL 2026IPL 2026: AB de Villiers questions MS Dhoni's role at CSK in IPL 2026, saying the legend must bat higher or risk playing for the wrong reasons as Sanju Samson's arrival reshapes Chennai's lineup.

IPL as big as international cricket: Ricky Ponting explains India's huge talent poolRicky Ponting believes the Indian Premier League plays a major role in shaping India's T20 success. The former Australia captain said IPL matches are as intense as international games and help prepare players for the national team.

Neymar focused on FIFA World Cup dream despite another Brazil squad snubNeymar's hopes of playing at the 2026 FIFA World Cup remain alive despite another Brazil squad snub. The Santos forward admitted he was disappointed after being left out of Brazil's upcoming friendlies in the United States.

Watch: Rohit Sharma in top gear in MI nets session ahead of IPL 2026IPL 2026: Rohit Sharma has already started turning heads ahead of IPL 2026. A viral video from Mumbai Indians' training session showed the former India captain going after the bowlers, raising excitement among fans before the new season.

A war crime: Afghanistan cricketers slam deadly Pak strikes on Kabul hospitalAfghanistan cricket stars Rashid Khan and Mohammad Nabi have strongly condemned reported Pakistani airstrikes in Kabul. The two players expressed grief and anger over civilian casualties after a hospital was reportedly struck during the attack.

KKR's fast bowling problem: 2024 winners head towards shaky start to IPL campaignKolkata Knight Riders enter the new IPL season with a fast-bowling unit already battered by injuries and uncertainty. With Harshit Rana unlikely to feature and Matheesha Pathirana still recovering, the defending champions suddenly look short of pace options and experience.

Pratika Rawal opens up on her deep bond with Test cricket: This is my formatPratika Rawal said that she considers Test cricket the ultimate measure of a cricketer's character, patience, and determination. She made her debut in the Day-Night Perth Test against Australia, scoring her maiden fifty.

Chennai Super Kings appoint ex-England wicketkeeper as fielding coach for IPL 2026Chennai Super Kings have appointed former England wicketkeeper-batter James Foster as their new fielding coach ahead of IPL 2026, adding an experienced coach to Stephen Fleming's support staff.

Liam Livingstone slams Brendon McCullum, Rob Key in sensational rant against ECBLiam Livingstone launched a blistering attack on England's team management, accusing Brendon McCullum and Rob Key of poor communication and a lack of support after being dropped from the white-ball setup.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
